betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Reflector§f:
A Fission Reflector is a multiblock component that bounces escaping neutrons back into the fuel, dramatically increasing neutron flux and reactor efficiency. It is the single biggest efficiency boost in compact reactor designs.

§6Crafting Recipes (CraftingRecipeHandler.java:231-232)§f:
Two reflector variants exist:
- §bBeryllium Reflector§f (meta 0): pattern "BGB / GFG / BGB" — Beryllium + Graphite + Steel Frame. Yields 2.
- §bLead Reflector§f (meta 1): pattern "LSL / SFS / LSL" — Lead + Steel + Steel Frame. Yields 2.

§6Neutron Return Rates§f:
- §bBeryllium Reflector§f: +50% neutron return, slight moderator behavior (slows neutrons in addition to reflecting)
- §bLead Reflector§f: +75% neutron return, very high heat capacity (acts as both reflector and heat sink)

§6Placement Rules§f:
- Place reflectors §9between fuel cells and reactor wall§f — they form a border around the fuel assemblies
- Cells along a wall should have a reflector on the wall face (the side touching the casing)
- In small reactors (≤6 cells), place reflectors on §9every fuel cell face§f that touches a wall
- In large reactors (≥9 cells), a single reflector border is sufficient

§6Why Reflectors Matter§f:
Reflectors are the §esingle biggest efficiency boost§f in compact reactor designs. They allow small reactors to achieve high criticality without large neutron-leakage losses. Without reflectors, a 3×3×3 reactor can lose 60-80% of its neutrons to leakage. With a full beryllium border, that drops to 20-30%.

§6Direct Descendants of Fission Reflector (TaskRetrieval)§f:
The task item is the Fission Reflector block itself (nuclearcraft:fission_reflector). Any meta variant satisfies the retrieval.

§6Synergy With Moderators§f:
Reflectors and moderators work together. Reflectors bounce neutrons back; moderators slow them down. The optimal arrangement is:
- Outer ring: Reflectors
- Inner ring: Moderators
- Center: Fuel Cells

This sandwiches the fuel between neutron-management layers, maximizing fission efficiency.

§6Prerequisites§f:
Requires q30047 (Moderator Lines) — the fuel cell + moderator scaffold must exist first.

§eTip§f: In small reactors (≤6 cells), place reflectors on §9every fuel cell face§f that touches a wall. The Reactor Simulator will show the flux boost clearly.

§eTip§f: For mid-game, Lead Reflectors are easier to mass-produce than Beryllium (Lead is more common). Reserve Beryllium Reflectors for high-tier compact reactors.

§eTip§f: Don't place a reflector where a moderator should go — they have different functions. Reflectors bounce neutrons outward; moderators slow them down in place. Use the Reactor Simulator to verify your layout.

§cWarning§f: An over-reflected reactor can become supercritical faster than expected. Always test new layouts with a low-enrichment fuel (LEU-235) and a small fuel inventory before scaling up.
